Alright, well welcome. I'm really glad to be here today. I'm Lori Haller. I think a lot of you probably think you know what I do, but I'm here to clear that up. I'm not just a graphic designer anymore. A long time ago when I did start I was just creating design like I said. I got into these big agencies and I was just making these really beautiful designs, meaningless, and everybody was so happy with the look, but they really didn't bring in the money like I realized when I concentrated on getting the attention of the reader. I worked with that copy, the visuals, aligning them, and really digging deep into either the product, the newsletter, maybe a magazine or a service, and then incorporating not just my beautiful design, but incorporating the exact type font, the exact color, the shape, the fold, all the right photos, strategy, researching the behavioral patterns of the people and audience that we're looking at. So instead of just doing all these little frivolous things just to make something beautiful, there's meaning behind it. There's clarity. When you do that and everything on this, I just passed out a sheet on this sheet on anything that you touch. That's when that copy and the visual and the meaning behind it is going to align properly so your audience can actually hear you, see you, follow along what you want them to do, and then sign up and send in the money. And really that's why we're all sitting here today. So I'm excited to show you some looks of what's working now and then we'll go over some of the design trends and helpful tips and tricks that you can put either into your web pages, email blasts, or print, print design. All right, so what's working? I love to do this everywhere I go when I speak. I kind of like to kick off with this because we all get so inundated with our day. We're so busy. We can't really look up and take a breath of fresh air and say, what's working? How are our humans changing? Are they more bored right now, which they are? Are they looking more on telephones? Are they looking on monitors? What are their feelings? So I try to bring this information to people that I speak with so that together we can all really hone in there and learn how to properly grasp the reader's attention, the audience, and force them to buy all of our products. So right now, what's working really well in print are MAG-A-LOGS, which are 8.5 by 11 mailers. I know you all have just reviewed a lot of the campaigns, but basically this is a MAG-A-LOG and this is working really well for us. 8.5 by 11. Sometimes it folds and it goes into a 6 by 9. I know you guys do some folding with envelopes and then a reply form for example. So this is working well as well as a slim gem, which is just a little smaller than that. It's more 6 by 10. I don't believe that you might have mailed with those before, but we can also go over some of those samples, but it's smaller and in the U.S. it allows us to mail more for a lot less. So that is a big decision-making factor is how much it costs to mail a piece and this is forcing us into certain kinds of design trends. Next are issue logs. Issue logs are just a mailer and instead of all those big fancy photos and headlines, you truly make it look like your report. Your report for example could look like a financial report and then intermittent in between this story or running copy, then after you've gained their trust, you start bringing them into wanting to buy the piece and you sell a little harder. So issue logs are working. Sample issues also that look and feel like you can trust them, that's ringing true. Right now in the United States, there's a lot less trust as you know and this will happen from time to time with humans. So we are working a lot harder with copy now in making us feel and look reliable and trustworthy. And then any campaign right now that is kind of winning, we've been able to expand it into a huge large newspaper look, a little glossy textured stock, not just a real low-end stock, maybe a little nicer, very large format and although that is so expensive, some of the larger companies are able to do that. So maybe when you go back to work on your products and projects, you might look at some of these different shapes that are tending to work well. Alright on the web, again, realistic words, feel and look. And I probably say that not just on the web but in print as well or emails, anything that we're touching, audio, video. Realistic right now is so critical. At the very beginning of the hot videos and fancy web, sensationalism was very popular but now people have gotten past that, they've seen it before so you really have to go right for the heart. Testing charts and graph styles, I'm going to show you some chart styles but people are getting tired in the investment sector and like the financial arena of the same old charts. So really watch it on some of your visuals, whether you're health, B2B, you know, all the things I'm going to tell you today, it really doesn't matter what you work on, this can perform well for anything but watch out for those charts and visuals that they're not the same old thing and try to reach outside or look outside the box, look on the internet, look to see what other people are doing, just to give people a little something different to look at when they're seeing numbers, columns and they're trying to align things and make some decisions on their own. Something that's working really well now is mobile and phone use. We'll talk about that in a second but I'd say back in September or not as many people were ordering off their phone and it's just been you know half of a year now and that is substantially increased in the US and I'm expecting it probably here as well. So on a copy and also on a design and we're looking at that, a phone is much smaller so we're actually writing copy for web for example, testing that, maybe we'll mail it but then when we decide to really blow it out or you know increase our volume on the web for example, we're then rewriting it, restructuring it just for the phone use. So it's a little less copy, it's a little faster, the photos are different so really consider the big picture now, you know many years ago it was just direct mail and then you kind of did a web thing. There's so many vehicles now that we're using so think why because that's where your bigger numbers are going to be. I will show you a couple samples today, something that I feel like our team was at the forefront of and that is small four to seven second animated gifts embedded into web design. Right at the pinnacle of where you kind of grab their attention. They see this interesting aligning gift that goes with the copy headline, the feeling and it just makes them want to keep going down the page. So I'd love to show those to you and see if perhaps you can incorporate into some of your pieces. Juicy looking reports and giveaways, people have gotten a little sloppy lately with just kind of slapping up any kind of report or give away. We're finding that these valuable reports where you get three free reports or two extra if you reply in the next 20 minutes, these are really making some push efforts towards the audience. So be careful when you are choosing the title and the content and designing the look, make sure they're valuable and make sure that they add and tie in to that main product so that it's all a series and they feel like I can't live without this report. You know I can't live without these gifts. I already wanted the product anyway so don't just become very lazy. Really keep pushing towards excellence there. Alright so selling power, here's a little more structured percentages of what we're seeing and that is from September November area last year 52% of the orders were coming in on phones that's what most of our clients around the you know area were reporting and so that kind of stirred up what we need to do differently in copy and visualness and ordering and then from January to right now we're hearing 56 to 78% increase and I think that's just going to keep going up. So as I heard around the room and then various places that I speak a lot of us work with the older you know criteria the 65 and up so we want to make sure that we're still not stuck like we were five years ago saying well most of our list that we're speaking to in health or finance are older they're not on the computer they don't know how to hear us or reach us you know we're not reaching them I think you need to start thinking differently now I certainly am and I think they are getting on the computer they're using one of your manuals now and I think they know how to get on the computer but I really think the tone and the words and the visuals must align with these older people but realize that they are on the computer now.
